summary: Well-designed workflow and script with excellent type hints (Python 3.12, explicit Mapping/Iterable/tuple types), proper error handling (FileNotFoundError/ValueError with exit codes), and clean abstractions (LicenseLookup class for mockability). Workflow structure is sound: pinned action refs (checkout@v4, setup-python@v5), least-privilege permissions (contents: read), sensible triggers (push/PR/dispatch/Monday 6am cron), job dependency (license-scan→unit-tests). Python script demonstrates TDD design with pure functions (parse_requirements, check_license) and clear separation of concerns. CRITICAL FLAW: Workflow references fixture files (fixtures/requirements.txt, fixtures/licenses.json, fixtures/license_lookup.json) that are not provided in deliverable, making license-scan job untestable. Without these files, the workflow fails at runtime. Setup step duplication (checkout+setup-python in both jobs) is acceptable GitHub Actions practice. One parse_requirements line (split(';',1)[0].split()[0]) handling environment markers could use clarification. Missing fixture files makes this deliverable incomplete and non-functional as-is.
